name: Corn Chowder
recipeIngredient: 1 large onion, cut into small dice
3 cups fresh corn kernels (from 5 or 6 large ears)
6 cups light chicken stock or low-salt chicken broth
1 pint cherry tomatoes, stemmed and quartered
2 cups firmly packed fresh basil leaves, cut into thin strips
recipeInstructions:
1. In a very large pot, sauté the onion and coriander in olive oil and butter over moderate heat for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
2. Add the wine and cook until the liquid has evaporated slightly, about 5 minutes.
3. Add the corn, chicken stock, and cream; bring to a boil over high heat.
4. Lower the heat slightly and simmer at a fast rate for 20 minutes, stirring frequently.
5. Add the tomatoes and basil, cooking for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve immediately.
Per serving: 612 calories, 9 grams protein, 43 grams carbohydrates, 45 grams fat, 23 grams saturated fat, 128 milligrams cholesterol, 86 milligrams sodium.
